items.title: Investors Expect ‘Sunshine and Rainbows’ This Earnings Season
items.description: Estimates are running up against sticky inflation and rising odds of interest rate hikes. With stocks near records, there’s little room for error.

items.title: Apple’s $600 Billion Rally Fueled by Traders Fleeing AI Selloff
items.description: Investors are flocking back to Apple Inc. as nervousness about artificial intelligence spending weighs on the stocks of chipmakers and cloud-computing giants.

items.title: Deutsche Bank Boosts Gulf Team With Standard Chartered Hires
items.description: Deutsche Bank AG is expanding its private banking business in Dubai with a series of hires from Standard Chartered Plc, further deepening its presence in the Middle East despite the regional conflict.

items.title: Saudi Tech Firm NourNet Is Said to Pick Goldman, HSBC for IPO

items.title: Fed’s Bowman Urges Global Financial Watchdog to be Flexible
items.description: The Federal Reserve’s top bank cop warned the Financial Stability Board against enforcing “strict rules” that are not suitable for a diverse group of countries, saying those efforts can erode the inte

items.title: India Trade Deficit Widens as Hormuz Uncertainty Clouds Outlook
items.description: India’s trade deficit widened in June, with uncertainty over the US-Iran conflict and shipping through the Strait of Hormuz continuing to cloud the outlook for global supply chains.

items.title: James Bond Studio Pinewood in Talks on Sterling Debt Sale
items.description: The UK film and TV studio behind the James Bond movies is meeting with investors ahead of a possible sale of sterling-denominated notes.

items.title: De Beers to Shutter South African Diamond Mine to Save Cash
items.description: Diamond giant De Beers plans to halt production at its South African mine for two years, as the one-time monopoly continues to wrestle with one of the deepest crises to ever hit the industry.

items.title: UAE Steps Up Moves to Help Term Buyers Avoid Hormuz Transits
items.description: Abu Dhabi National Oil Co. is broadening the options for long-term customers to take offshore oil from outside the Strait of Hormuz, stepping up efforts that enable buyers to circumvent the chokepoint

items.title: Indonesia Avoids S&P Rating Outlook Cut in Win for Prabowo
items.description: Indonesia retained its investment-grade rating and stable outlook at S&P Global Ratings, a reprieve for President Prabowo Subianto and his fiscal policy credibility after a bond market selloff in June

items.title: Germany’s Smag Sinks in Debut as Defense Stock Appetite Ebbs
items.description: Smag Mobile Antenna Masts AG shares tumbled as much as 33% below the initial public offering price in their German trading debut, marking the latest sign of waning demand for defense stocks in Europe.

items.title: European Stocks Steady as Energy Gains Offset Tech Weakness
items.description: European stocks were muted as declines in technology shares were offset by gains in the energy sector, which was lifted by higher oil prices.
